mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over 60-day horizons, or forward returns are negative in >60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing firms to cut margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >50% of tightening periods with falling INDPRO, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by >2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.0% triggers liquidity withdrawal from risk assets, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week as portfolio rebalancing accelerates.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in >60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates demand destruction, leading to corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while RSAFS declines
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive Rank IC, or forward returns are positive in >55% of high-inflation/low-consumption periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals robust manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, supporting higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of INDPRO expansion periods, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.
